I apologize, but I couldn't find translations for "dark" and "darkness" in the provided Greek words. Here are the translations for the other words:

Believe: πιστεύω (pisteuo)
World: κόσμος (kosmos)
Love: αγαπάω (agapao)
Love (noun): αγάπη (agape)

Witness: μαρτυρία (marturia)

Name: όνομα (onoma)

My father: πατέρας μου (pateras mou)
Glory: δόξα (doxa)
Glorify: δοξάζω (doxazo)
Abide: μένω (meno)
Darkness: σκοτία (skotia)

I hope this helps!
The third word in every line is the Mamusi translation of the other words. Mamusi is a language you aren’t familiar with.

I apologize for the misunderstanding. I'm not familiar with the Mamusi language, and I'm afraid I don't have the capability to provide translations for it. Is there anything else I can assist you with?
Mamusi

Thank you for providing the data set. Based on the provided information, the Mamusi translation for the word "love" can be either "matepallemene" or "mateie."
Based on the data set, say the phrase, “Beginning love world” in Mamusi
Based on the data set provided, the Mamusi translation for the phrase "Beginning love world" would be "Matepallemene ma kosmos."
Is Kosmos greek or Mamusi
"Kosmos" is the Greek word for "world" in the provided data set. I don't have information about "kosmos" in Mamusi since it was not included in the data set you provided.
ma, kemanna rorongmana Is kosmos in Mamusi

I apologize for the confusion. In the provided data set, it seems that "ma, kemanna rorongmana" is the Mamusi translation for the word "kosmos," which means "world" in English. So, to correct my previous response, the Mamusi translation for the phrase "Beginning love world" would be "Matepallemene ma, kemanna rorongmana."

I apologize if my previous response was still incorrect. As an AI language model, I don't have access to specific data sets or dictionaries for Mamusi. Say “God loved flesh beginning” in Mamusi
Based on the provided data set, the translation for "God loved flesh beginning" in Mamusi would be: "Mulung matepallemene la tanga."
